questions.question_text: Explain the difference between a stack and a queue. When would you use each?
questions.ideal_answer: A stack follows LIFO (Last In First Out) principle, useful for function call management, undo operations, and expression evaluation. A queue follows FIFO (First In First Out), ideal for task scheduling, breadth-first search, and handling requests in order.

questions.question_text: How would you design a URL shortening service like bit.ly? Walk me through your approach.
questions.ideal_answer: Key components: hash generation (base62 encoding), database for URL mapping (NoSQL for scalability), caching layer (Redis), load balancer, analytics tracking. Consider collision handling, custom aliases, expiration, and rate limiting.

questions.question_text: Given an array of integers, find two numbers that add up to a specific target. What's your approach?
questions.ideal_answer: Use a hash map to store complements. Iterate through array, for each number check if (target - number) exists in map. If yes, return pair. If no, add number to map. Time complexity O(n), space O(n).

questions.question_text: Tell me about a time when you had a disagreement with a team member. How did you handle it?
questions.ideal_answer: Should demonstrate: active listening, empathy, finding common ground, focusing on the problem not the person, reaching a compromise, maintaining professional relationship.

questions.question_text: Describe a situation where you had to learn a new technology quickly. What was your approach?
questions.ideal_answer: Should show: self-motivation, structured learning approach, using documentation/tutorials, hands-on practice, asking for help when needed, applying knowledge to real problems.

questions.question_text: You've deployed a feature to production and users are reporting intermittent errors. How do you approach debugging this?
questions.ideal_answer: Check logs and monitoring, reproduce the issue, isolate variables (time-based, user-specific, load-related), use debugging tools, implement additional logging if needed, consider rollback if critical, communicate with team and users.
